title: D->C vocab mismatch — biz-language decision, impl-language topic
topic: idempotent payment processing

decisions.description: Customers cannot be billed twice for the same order
JWT tokens expire after 60 minutes
PII in logs must be redacted

note: If the LLM bridges biz language ('billed twice') to impl language ('idempotent payment'), it picks billing. The original M1 failure mode predicts a miss; recall here is the empirical recall rate against the v0.10.x architecture.

title: Synonym pair — throttling ↔ rate limiting
topic: API throttling middleware for external clients

decisions.description: Limit each customer to 100 outbound API calls per minute to protect downstream vendors
Apply VAT for EU customers based on billing address
Idle sessions expire after 12 hours

note: Synonym pair: 'throttling' (topic) vs 'rate limit' (decision). LLM must recognize these refer to the same concept.

title: Synonym pair — soft delete ↔ archive
topic: implement soft delete for cancelled orders

decisions.description: Cancelled orders must be archived, not erased — finance needs the audit trail for 7 years
Admin actions require MFA
Daily digest emails sent at 09:00 UTC

note: Synonym pair: 'soft delete' (topic) vs 'archive' (decision). Both express the same retain-the-row pattern using different jargon.

title: Abstraction shift — policy (GDPR) → implementation (cron job)
topic: nightly PII purge cron job for closed accounts

decisions.description: Customer personal data must be removed within 30 days of account closure (GDPR Art. 17)
Invoices generated on the first of each month
Production logs default to INFO; DEBUG is opt-in per request

note: Policy-level decision (GDPR retention) framed in regulatory language; topic is the implementation mechanism (cron). LLM must bridge legal-policy vocabulary to ops-implementation vocabulary.

title: Abstraction shift — policy (trust boundary) → implementation (validator)
topic: input validation schema for webhook payload handler

decisions.description: Data from external integrations must never be trusted without explicit verification
Form errors render inline next to the failing field
Hard bounces unsubscribe the address after one occurrence

note: Policy-level decision (trust boundary) → implementation-level topic (validation schema). LLM must connect the abstract 'never trust external data' principle to the concrete validation step.

title: Abstraction shift — policy (perf SLA) → implementation (cache warming)
topic: Redis cache warming step in deploy pipeline

decisions.description: Page loads for logged-in users must not exceed 500ms at p95
Default currency is USD; per-customer override via account settings
Password reset links expire after 1 hour

note: Performance SLA (decision) vs cache-warming implementation (topic). LLM must reason that cache warming exists to meet the latency SLA.

title: Cross-domain — security policy ↔ UX feature
topic: session timeout countdown UI banner

decisions.description: Idle sessions must auto-expire after 15 minutes to comply with SOC2 CC6.7
Card authorizations captured within 7 days or released
Push notifications respect user-set quiet hours

note: Cross-domain wording: security-compliance decision (SOC2) vs UX implementation topic (countdown banner). The countdown UI exists because of the compliance constraint — LLM must bridge.

title: Cross-domain — infrastructure topic ↔ product performance decision
topic: CDN edge caching configuration for product images

decisions.description: Product page first-meaningful-paint must complete within 2 seconds globally
Third-party OAuth providers limited to Google and GitHub
Free trial period is 14 days, no credit card required

note: Infra-language topic (CDN edge caching) vs product-language decision (page load time globally). LLM must bridge 'CDN edge caching' as the mechanism for the 'globally fast' product requirement.

title: Ungrounded decision — visible only via skill judgment over history
topic: permission middleware client check

decisions.description: Permission checks always run server-side; clients must never assume their own grant set
Pro-rate refunds on plan downgrade
Session cookies expire after 12 hours of inactivity

note: Ungrounded decision (no binds_to edge) — region-anchored lookup cannot find it. Only the skill's history-reasoning step can surface it. Tests whether status='ungrounded' biases the LLM against picking it.

title: Ungrounded policy — PII redaction in logs
topic: structured logging for payment webhook handler

decisions.description: Credit card numbers in logs must always be replaced with last-four-only
Failed deploys rollback automatically after 3 consecutive 5xx samples
Session state stored in Redis with 24-hour TTL

note: Ungrounded PII-redaction policy (load-bearing for compliance). Region-anchored lookup misses it because no binds_to edge exists yet. Skill must recognize that a payment webhook handler writes logs and the PII rule applies.

title: Ungrounded policy — dual-control approval for sensitive admin actions
topic: admin tool for user role assignment

decisions.description: Role grants at level=manager or above require dual-control approval from a second admin
Three plan tiers: Free, Pro, Enterprise
Activity digest cadence is daily by default, configurable to weekly

note: Ungrounded auth policy: dual-control requirement that a 'role assignment' admin tool would otherwise quietly bypass. Tests whether the skill surfaces policy-level constraints that scope the implementation.

title: Ungrounded policy — raw event retention
topic: S3 lifecycle rule for raw event payloads

decisions.description: Raw event payloads must be deleted after 90 days; only aggregates persist longer
JWT tokens signed with HS256
Dashboard charts refresh every 30 seconds while the tab is focused

note: Ungrounded retention policy. Topic is the implementation surface (S3 lifecycle), decision is the policy that scopes it. Skill must connect lifecycle-rule work to retention policy.

title: Ungrounded cross-cutting — distributed-trace propagation
topic: trace ID propagation through async Celery tasks

decisions.description: Every customer-affecting request must be traceable end-to-end across all services for incident response
Invoices delivered as PDF attachments
Outbound email routed through SES with bounce tracking

note: Ungrounded cross-cutting observability decision. Topic ('trace ID propagation') is the concrete implementation; decision is the policy ('traceable end-to-end'). Tests cross-cutting policy retrieval.

title: Ungrounded cross-cutting — outbound webhook retry policy
topic: retry-with-backoff implementation for outbound customer webhooks

decisions.description: Transient downstream failures must not surface as customer-visible errors
MFA grace period is 30 days for new accounts
Refunds permitted within 30 days of original charge

note: Ungrounded reliability policy. Retry-with-backoff is the textbook implementation of the 'no customer-visible transient errors' principle. Skill must bridge.

title: Ungrounded cross-cutting — internal observability over user reports
topic: p95 latency dashboard for API endpoints

decisions.description: Service health must be measurable independently of user-submitted incident reports
Proration calculated to the second, not the day
SMS notifications require explicit per-channel opt-in

note: Ungrounded observability policy that the dashboard literally implements. Tests whether the skill connects 'we need internal health signals' to a concrete dashboard build-out.

title: Ungrounded edge — audit trail for system-initiated actions
topic: background job that updates user usage counters

decisions.description: System-initiated actions must record the originating user context for compliance audit
All charts rendered via Recharts for consistency
Unsubscribe links honored within 24 hours

note: Ungrounded audit edge case: background jobs that modify user state need the originating context for audit. Easy to miss if the skill only reasons about 'usage counters' lexically.

title: Skill should NOT drill into feature groups unrelated to the topic
topic: frontend dashboard color palette refresh

decisions.description: Customers cannot be billed twice for the same order
JWT tokens expire after 60 minutes
Webhook payloads use snake_case JSON

note: No feature group is plausibly relevant to a CSS/color refresh task. If the LLM picks any group it's a false-fire — it's surfacing decisions that have no bearing on the work.

title: Pure styling work — no policy connection

decisions.description: Default currency is USD with per-customer override
API versioning via URL path /v1, /v2
Role hierarchy is owner > admin > member > viewer

note: Pure UI styling. No decision in the ledger plausibly governs theme color logic. Any pick is a false-fire.

Transactional emails use the Postmark template engine
Role assignments require admin role on the actor

note: Dep bump with no behavior change in scope. None of the seeded decisions govern react-query usage. Any pick is over-fetching.

decisions.description: OAuth providers: Google, GitHub, Microsoft only
Refunds permitted within 30 days of original charge
Modals dismissable via ESC and outside-click

Invoices localized to the customer's billing-address language
Deprecated API versions sunset 12 months after announcement

Plan changes take effect at the start of the next billing cycle
Free-tier accounts limited to 3 pending invitations at a time
